source.title: Mesoscale cortex-wide neural dynamics predict self-initiated actions in mice several seconds prior to movement

confidence_rationale: Widefield calcium imaging showing structured multi-second pre-movement dynamics distributed across cortex, including inhibition of motor cortex starting ~5s before self-initiated action. Establishes that commit-precursor signals are distributed (not a single token) and extend several seconds before movement -- constraining the timescale window for any commit token. Confidence reduced by species gap (mouse vs. human) and absence of direct commitment/inhibition paradigm.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Cortical activity in mice shows structured variance changes 3-5 seconds prior to self-initiated lever pulls; motor cortex is initially inhibited (~5s before) then activated (~2s before); action prediction is best with distributed multi-area rather than single-area signals. These dynamics are structured beyond random neural fluctuations.
mapping.ree_translation: The multi-second premovement dynamics suggest that the commit precursor is not a single moment but a trajectory. In REE terms, the E3 planning loop must begin encoding a 'warming up to commit' signal several seconds before gate opening. This constrains the minimal commit token: it cannot be instantiated at a single moment without losing the trajectory information that allows accurate attribution of when the decision was effectively irreversible. The inhibition-then-activation sequence in motor cortex maps loosely onto REE's pre-commit simulation phase (suppression of premature execution) followed by commit-gate opening (activation enabling movement). The distributed prediction advantage implies that the commit token should aggregate signals across multiple subsystems, not just the gate-opening locus.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Mouse widefield calcium imaging cannot resolve the cognitive-level planning signals that REE's E3 represents. The lever-pull paradigm involves a simple reward-motivated movement with no ethical or multi-option planning component. The 5-second window observed here may be a lower bound for mouse motor actions; REE's planning horizon is substantially longer and more abstract. The species transfer from mouse to human-analogous agent is a significant additional assumption.
mapping.source_context: Mouse widefield calcium imaging, self-initiated lever pull paradigm, reward-motivated behavior, healthy adult mice

confidence_components.notes: High-quality imaging data with careful controls for random-process confounds. Transfer risk elevated by species gap and task simplicity. The main REE-relevant finding (distributed, multi-second pre-commit trajectory) is suggestive but not directly probative of what a minimal token contract must contain.
